§6Dense Energy Cell§f

§a8× the storage of a regular Energy Cell.§f

The §bDense Energy Cell§f stores §e1,600,000 AE§f — §e8×§f the capacity of a regular §bEnergy Cell§f. Essential for large networks with heavy crafting loads.

§6Specifications§f:
§b•§f Capacity: §e1,600,000 AE§f
§b•§f §e8× the capacity§f of a standard Energy Cell
§b•§f No channel cost (passes through power)
§b•§f Stackable: multiple dense cells stack their buffer

§6Placement§f:
Place on any cable in the network. The cell accepts power from any side and feeds power to any side. The cell §9does not consume a channel§f.

§6Crafting Recipe§f:
§b•§f §b4× Energy Cell§f
§b•§f §b4× Fluix Crystal§f
§b•§f §b1× Iron Block§f

§6Strategic Role§f:
The §bDense Energy Cell§f is the §emid-game energy buffer§f. For networks with §bMolecular Assemblers§f, §bCrafting CPUs§f, or §bMass storage drives§f, you need several dense cells to handle peak demand.

§eTip§f: Place 2-3 §bDense Energy Cells§f in dedicated §9power rooms§f to keep the network stable during heavy crafting bursts.
